_excludedOrgs: Durham Rescue Mission — do not re-add. Removed 2026-04-22 per Commissioner Allam's direction. If the resources list is ever rebuilt from scratch or re-scraped, this organization must remain excluded.

categories.title: Housing Help
categories.intro: Rent help, home repair grants, and paths to ownership for Durham neighbors.

cards.name: City of Durham Home Repair Program
cards.desc: Income-eligible homeowners can get grants or loans for critical home repairs through the City's Neighborhood Improvement Services.

cards.name: Durham County Emergency Rental Assistance
cards.desc: Durham County DSS offers short-term emergency assistance for rent and utilities to qualified households.

cards.name: Durham Community Land Trustees (DCLT)
cards.desc: Permanently affordable homeownership for Durham neighbors with low to moderate incomes. ADA accessible homes throughout Durham.

cards.name: NC 211
cards.desc: Dial 2-1-1 or visit nc211.org to be connected to housing resources, emergency assistance, and more across North Carolina.

categories.title: Food & Nutrition
categories.intro: SNAP, food pantries, and hot meals for Durham neighbors facing food insecurity.

cards.name: SNAP (Food Stamps)
cards.desc: Apply for Food and Nutrition Services (SNAP) through Durham County Department of Social Services.

cards.name: Food Bank of Central & Eastern NC
cards.desc: Provides food through partner pantries throughout Durham and across central and eastern North Carolina.

cards.name: Inter-Faith Food Shuttle
cards.desc: Mobile and fixed-site food access across the Triangle, including Durham neighborhoods.

cards.name: Durham County Community Food Resources
cards.desc: Durham County's directory of local food pantries, hot meal sites, and nutrition programs. Find what's near you.

categories.title: Pregnancy & New Parents
categories.intro: Nutrition support, home visiting programs, and childcare resources for expecting and new parents in Durham.

cards.name: WIC: Durham County Health Dept
cards.desc: Free nutrition support, healthy food, breastfeeding help, and health referrals for pregnant women, new moms, and kids under 5.

cards.name: Durham's Partnership for Children
cards.desc: Smart Start programs for early childhood development, parenting support, and childcare help for Durham families.

cards.name: Nurse-Family Partnership
cards.desc: Free home-visiting program pairing first-time moms with a registered nurse from pregnancy through the child's second birthday.

cards.name: Diaper Bank of NC
cards.desc: Free diapers and period supplies for Durham families in need. Durham distribution at Urban Ministries (410 Liberty St).

cards.name: El Centro Hispano
cards.desc: Bilingual support for Latino families including health navigation, parenting programs, and community resources.

cards.name: Durham Cooperative Extension Giving Closet
cards.desc: Free infant, toddler, and children's clothing (ages 0–5), maternity clothing, baby equipment, and diapers based on availability. 721 Foster St, Durham. Mon–Thu 9am–4:30pm.

categories.title: Reproductive Health
categories.intro: Licensed medical providers offering reproductive health care in Durham, including contraception, abortion services, fertility care, and STI testing.

cards.name: Planned Parenthood Durham Health Center
cards.desc: Licensed health center offering birth control, STI testing, abortion care, pregnancy testing, and sexual health services. 105 Newsom St, Durham.

cards.name: Durham County Health: Family Planning
cards.desc: Low-cost and sliding-scale reproductive health services including contraception, STI testing, and pregnancy testing through the county health department (Title X funded).

cards.name: Carolina Abortion Fund
cards.desc: Confidential financial assistance and practical support for NC and SC neighbors seeking abortion care. Call to request funding.

cards.name: National Abortion Federation Hotline
cards.desc: Free, confidential referrals to quality abortion providers and financial assistance for anyone in the US. English and Spanish. Call 1-800-772-9100.

cards.name: Duke Fertility Center
cards.desc: Comprehensive fertility evaluation and treatment including IVF, IUI, egg freezing, and reproductive endocrinology for Durham area neighbors.

categories.title: Domestic Violence & Safety
categories.note: You are not alone. These organizations offer confidential support.

cards.name: Durham Crisis Response Center
cards.desc: Durham's primary domestic violence resource. 24/7 crisis line, emergency shelter, counseling, court advocacy, and safety planning.

cards.name: National Domestic Violence Hotline
cards.desc: Free, confidential, 24/7 support for anyone experiencing domestic violence. Call 1-800-799-7233 or text START to 88788.

cards.name: Legal Aid of NC
cards.desc: Free legal help for domestic violence survivors including protective orders, divorce, and custody matters.

cards.name: El Centro Hispano
cards.desc: Bilingual services and advocacy for Spanish-speaking survivors of domestic violence in Durham.

categories.title: Immigration Support
categories.note: Know your rights. All organizations listed provide confidential support regardless of immigration status.

cards.name: Siembra NC
cards.desc: Durham-based immigrant rights organization offering know-your-rights training, rapid response, and community organizing. Call or text to report ICE activity in your area or workplace wage theft. Available regardless of immigration status.

cards.name: El Centro Hispano
cards.desc: Immigration assistance, legal referrals, ESL classes, and community services for Durham's Latino community.

cards.name: Legal Aid of NC: Immigration
cards.desc: Free immigration legal help for qualifying low-income people in NC, including DACA, visas, and more.

categories.title: Mental Health & Crisis Response
categories.intro: Help is here. These Durham programs offer crisis lines, counseling, and mental health services for neighbors in need.

cards.name: HEART Program
cards.desc: Durham's Holistic Empathetic Assistance and Response Team sends mental health clinicians (not police) to non-violent behavioral health calls. Call 911 or the non-emergency line and ask for HEART.

cards.name: 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline
cards.desc: Free, confidential crisis support 24/7. Call or text 988 to reach a trained counselor.

cards.name: Alliance Health
cards.desc: Manages mental health, substance use, and developmental disability services for Durham County neighbors.

cards.name: Durham County Behavioral Health Services
cards.desc: Durham County's Department of Health and Human Services provides mental health referrals and crisis services for county neighbors.

categories.title: Shelter & Unhoused Support
categories.intro: Emergency shelter, warming and cooling centers, and support services for Durham neighbors who are unhoused or at risk.
categories.note: For the most current shelter availability, call Durham Entry Point: 984-287-8313 or dial 2-1-1.

cards.name: Urban Ministries of Durham
cards.desc: Emergency shelter for adults, 3 free meals per day, food pantry, clothing closet, and case management. 410 Liberty St, Durham. Open 365 days a year.

cards.name: Durham Winter Shelters
cards.desc: City-coordinated warming shelters activate when temperatures drop to 32°F (or 35°F with precipitation), Nov 1–Mar 31. Includes family shelters and adult-only locations. Call 984-287-8313 for locations.

cards.name: Durham Cooling Centers (Summer)
cards.desc: During heat emergencies the City opens cooling centers including libraries, rec centers, and community spaces. Check the City's website or call 919-560-1200 during summer heat events.

cards.name: Families Moving Forward Durham
cards.desc: Emergency family shelter for families with children experiencing homelessness in Durham.

cards.name: NC 211
cards.desc: Dial 2-1-1 anytime for up-to-date shelter availability across Durham, or search online at nc211.org.

categories.title: Free & Low-Cost Clinics
categories.intro: These Durham clinics offer sliding-scale and free care regardless of insurance status or ability to pay.

cards.name: Lincoln Community Health Center
cards.desc: Federally Qualified Health Center serving Durham since 1971. Primary care, dental, behavioral health, and pediatrics on a sliding-fee scale. 1301 Fayetteville St, Durham.

cards.name: Durham County Health Department
cards.desc: Sliding-scale clinical services including immunizations, STI testing, family planning, WIC, and communicable disease care. No one turned away for inability to pay.

cards.name: NC Medicaid Enrollment
cards.desc: See if you qualify for free or low-cost health coverage through NC Medicaid or NC Health Choice. Apply through Durham County DSS or online.

cards.name: NC 211
cards.desc: Call 2-1-1 to be connected to free and sliding-scale health clinics near you in Durham County.

categories.title: Emergency & Utility Assistance
categories.intro: Short-term help with rent, utilities, and other essentials for Durham neighbors in a financial crisis.

cards.name: Durham County DSS Emergency Assistance
cards.desc: Short-term help with rent, utilities, and other essentials for qualifying households facing a crisis.

cards.name: NC 211
cards.desc: Dial 2-1-1 anytime to find emergency assistance, utility help, food, shelter, and more in Durham.

cards.name: Durham Community Action
cards.desc: Community Action Agency serving Durham with energy assistance (LIHEAP), emergency aid, and workforce support programs.

cards.name: Duke Energy Share
cards.desc: Duke Energy's bill assistance program for customers who are struggling to pay their electric bill.

categories.title: Legal Help
categories.intro: Free and low-cost legal help for Durham neighbors: housing, family law, immigration, benefits, and more.

cards.name: Legal Aid of NC
cards.desc: Free civil legal help for low-income North Carolinians: housing, family law, immigration, benefits, and more.

cards.name: NC Free Legal Answers
cards.desc: Submit civil legal questions online and get free answers from volunteer NC attorneys. Easy and confidential.

cards.name: Durham County Courthouse Law Library
cards.desc: Free public access to legal research resources at the Durham County Courthouse.

categories.title: Parks & Greenways
categories.intro: Durham has over 70 parks, dozens of miles of greenways, and free recreation centers across the county and city. Get outside!

cards.name: Durham Parks, Recreation & Open Space
cards.desc: Parks, greenways, nature programs, recreation centers, pools, and open space across Durham. Includes Eno River access, Little River Regional Park, and more.

cards.name: City of Durham Parks & Recreation
cards.desc: City-operated parks, recreation centers, pools, athletic fields, playgrounds, and programs across Durham neighborhoods.

cards.name: Durham Greenway Trails
cards.desc: Durham's greenway network connects neighborhoods, parks, and downtown with paved trails open to walkers, joggers, and cyclists. Find trail maps and access points on the City's site.

cards.name: Eno River State Park
cards.desc: A beloved Durham-area state park with hiking, swimming, and paddling along the Eno River. Free to enter. Multiple access points throughout Durham and Orange counties.

cards.name: Durham Recreation Centers
cards.desc: Durham's recreation centers offer fitness equipment, classes, and programs at low or no cost. Financial assistance is available for neighbors who need it.

categories.title: Public Libraries
categories.intro: Durham County Library branches are free for all Durham County neighbors! Get a library card with any proof of Durham County address. All branches offer books, e-books, computers, Wi-Fi, and community programs.

cards.name: Main Library
cards.desc: Durham County's flagship library. 300 N. Roxboro St., Durham, NC 27701. (919) 560-0100. Books, computers, meeting rooms, and the largest collection in the system.

cards.name: South Regional Library
cards.desc: Serving south Durham. 4505 S. Alston Avenue, Durham, NC 27713. (919) 560-7409. Full library services, community meeting space, and Spanish-language materials.

cards.name: East Regional Library
cards.desc: Serving east Durham. 211 Lick Creek Lane, Durham, NC 27703. (919) 560-0862. Full library services with community programs.

cards.name: North Regional Library
cards.desc: Serving north Durham. 221 Milton Road, Durham, NC 27712. (919) 560-0236. Full library services and community programs.

cards.name: Southwest Regional Library
cards.desc: Serving southwest Durham. 3605 Shannon Road, Durham, NC 27707. (919) 560-8593. Full library services with community programs.

cards.name: Stanford L. Warren Branch Library
cards.desc: Serving the Hayti/Fayetteville Street corridor. 1201 Fayetteville Street, Durham, NC 27707. (919) 560-0270. Full library services and community programs.

cards.name: Bragtown Branch Library
cards.desc: Serving north Durham's Bragtown neighborhood. 3200 Dearborn Drive, Durham, NC 27704. (919) 560-0210. Full library services and community programs.

cards.name: Durham County Library: Digital Resources
cards.desc: Free e-books, audiobooks, digital magazines, and online databases available 24/7 with your free library card. Access from home on any device.
